video_title: Top #DDD Fall Flavor Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Catalina's
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Columbus, Ohio. I'd hunter. What do you feel like eating today? You know dad? I'm feeling some breakfast food. I know you're not a fan of that. So we can don't worry. Don't worry. I'm taking you a place. It does breakfast and lunch. Okay, they're doing something that I used to eat in my childhood, but they're just doing a little bit different different good different bad. Oh different great. I mean when their motto is peace love and pain cake balls, you know, we got to check it out. This is Catalina's to

dishes.dish_name: Dulce de Leche Pancake Balls with Sweet and Spicy Bacon
dishes.description: Pancake batter made with unbleached flour, corn flour, milk, baking powder, salt, and eggs. Cooked in an Aebleskiver pan with clarified butter, filled with dulce de leche. Served with powdered sugar, sweet and spicy bacon (cayenne, brown sugar, condensed milk), and local maple syrup.
dishes.guy_response: Outstanding one. This is not an able skipper. I told you pancake ball. It's kind of glazed over the top. Yeah, a little bit of a hard texture on top, but you get it all the way around then the little dulce de leche inside of it. Not too sweet. And then you dip into the maple syrup local local. Yeah, and then you refresh your palate with spicy fatty bacon. It's awesome. Come on. The dulce de leche gets soaked into the pancake balls and it's one juicy bite very crushable the sweet and spicy bacon really flavorful salty sweet and texture for me. It's my jam.

dishes.description: A variation of the pancake ball made with apple butter.

restaurants.name: Rose Villa Southern Table and Bar
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Ormond Beach, Florida. Well, do you think really great southern food that's been chefed up? Well, you will after you check out this joint. This is Rose Villa southern table and bar.

dishes.dish_name: Bourbon Barbecue Pork Chop with Tree Fruit Chutney and Goat Cheese Grits
dishes.description: Bone-in pork chop brined in salt, brown sugar, water, and root beer for 24 hours. Glazed with bourbon barbecue sauce (ketchup, brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, bourbon, molasses, root beer, coffee, Worcestershire, garlic, onion, chili powder, mustard). Served with tree fruit chutney (peaches, apples, sun-dried cranberries, garlic, ginger, apple cider vinegar, sugar, lemon juice, red pepper flakes, dry mustard, turmeric, coriander, cinnamon) and goat cheese grits (half water, half milk, butter, heavy cream, goat cheese, salt).
dishes.guy_response: The chutney is the kiss here. The chop itself is great. It's tender. It's got great flavor in it, but the sweetness and a bit of the acidity from the chutney. Richest of the chop with a good sear on that and then the tang of the goat cheese grits. I mean the medley of what you put together when you get that perfect bite of all three dynamite. The bourbon barbecue pork chop the only thing I like better than drinking bourbon is eating food cooked with bourbon. It just provides such a richness house workshop really really good.

restaurants.name: In a Pickle
restaurants.guy_intro: We're here Boston and I said Sam you want to go to lunch? I know you got a couple shows you're doing and I'm shooting Triple D. What do you say? I say it better be good guy. I know I know it's in a pickle. Wait a minute. We've been in a pickle half-way for our lives, I know but that's the name of the joint breakfast and lunch in a pickle. I'm hungry.

dishes.description: Homemade pumpkin bread (eggs, oil, water, pumpkin, flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, baking powder, baking soda, cloves, salt, sugar) baked, then sliced and dipped in egg custard (eggs, light cream, vanilla, cinnamon). Cooked, topped with sweet cream cheese (cream cheese, butter, powdered sugar, vanilla), powdered sugar, allspice, and dulce de leche caramel sauce.
dishes.guy_response: I enjoy that more than I enjoy a typical dessert because you got a savory factor to it. Pumpkin bread baked that way on the sheet. You're getting a little bit of the crust with this soft, moist center to it. And you're getting all the spices. But you get the tang of the cream cheese coming through, so you get a little bit of the sweet and sour combo. When you're taking that much time to make such a simple dish the next level, that's why you're doing so well.

restaurants.name: Joy's Once Upon a Time
restaurants.guy_intro: A single mom has got an all-female staff, and her restaurant is her way, her style, her energy, her attitude. It's kind of like right out of a storybook. This is Joy's Once Upon a Time.

dishes.dish_name: Turkey Artichoke Panini with Fresh Basil Dressing
dishes.description: Sous vide turkey breast seasoned with salt and pepper. Served on rustic bread with artichoke tapenade (artichokes, green stuffed olives, Parmesan, Osseago, tomato, garlic, parsley, salt, pepper, lemon rind, lemon juice, California olive oil, basil), tomatoes, Swiss cheese, and fresh basil dressing (grape seed oil, California olive oil, local honey, brown rice vinegar, white wine vinegar, water, onion, garlic, oregano, dry mustard, Italian parsley, basil, salt). Pressed in a panini press.
dishes.guy_response: I'm biting into this sandwich, and it took me back to Ferndale as a 10-year-old kid. The bread, the tapenade, the turkey, these are the kind of flavors I love. You either have to do each one of those pieces and do them exactly right, or it's not going to work. You do it all right, with great ingredients, and the right technique, you're going to get this. Delicious.

restaurants.name: Jimmy's Down the Street
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm Guy Fieri, and we're rolling out, looking for America's greatest diners, drive-ins, and dives. Like this place here. Been doing business for over 50 years, and is now called Jimmy's Down the Street.

dishes.description: Dough made with yeast, milk, warm water, salt, sugar, eggs, cinnamon, butter, and flour. Proofed, rolled with butter, brown sugar, and cinnamon. Caramel sauce made with butter, caramel syrup, and brown sugar. Topped with pecans. Baked at 325 degrees for about 25 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Such great texture to the dough. The sugar and the caramel starts to almost candy up right on the outside of it. The pecans are rocking, and it's not just a little sprinkle dinkle. That's a fish full of pecan. Awesome. Nice job.

restaurants.name: Commonwealth
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Modesto, California, about an hour south of Sacramento. You're going to love this story. Two high school buddies that live in separate parts of the state decide to move back to Modesto because it's a great place to raise their families. And they also decide, hey, why don't we open up a craft beer joint and make scratch-made food? You've got to love the American dream. This is commonwealth.

dishes.dish_name: Craft Tacos (Bami, Barbecue Pork, Buffalo Chicken)
dishes.description: A plate with three different tacos: a Bami taco, a barbecue pork taco, and a buffalo chicken taco.
dishes.guy_response: Honestly, I can't decide which one to start with. Loving it. Absolutely off the charts.

dishes.description: Waffle batter (all-purpose flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, kosher salt, eggs, buttermilk, vanilla, butter) cooked. Cut in half, filled with cheese, ham (cooked in maple mustard butter), and topped with a fried egg. Served with home fries.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, boy. The waffle should be illegal. It actually is. It should be in before you can eat that thing. Nice job, guys.

restaurants.name: Lake Effect Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: So here I am, Buffalo, New York, about five miles northeast of Lake Erie, at a joint that was once a diner and then changed into a Chinese restaurant, well, then transplanted from Pennsylvania, and then turned back into a diner. And guess what? That's not even the amazing part. These guys, they're curing their own ham, they're corning their own beef, and they're smoking their own bacon. This is Lake Effect Diner.

dishes.dish_name: House-Cured Smoked Ham with Red-Eye Gravy
dishes.description: Boneless pork leg cured in a brine (water, dried cloves, black peppercorns, dry mustard, light brown sugar, maple syrup, sodium nitrate, kosher salt) for 12-14 days. Smoked over mesquite charcoal, apple, and hickory woods for 3 hours. Served with red-eye gravy (smoked ham bones, pan juices, mirepoix, black peppercorn, kosher salt, Frank's hot sauce, fresh thyme, coffee). Served with eggs and home fries.
dishes.guy_response: This is very flavorful. Tender ham for sure. I feel like I have a side of ham with some ham. Being raised eating red-eye gravy, I've seen a lot of different variations. I do think that I would like to have a cup of red-eye gravy in a choc. Awesome, bub. Good job.

restaurants.name: Blackbird Wood Fire
restaurants.guy_intro: Six and a half years of making wood-fired pizzas their way. This is Blackbird Wood Fire.

dishes.description: Pizza dough (salt, water, all-purpose flour, yeast, bread flour) mixed, balled, and proofed for 24 hours in a cooler. Topped with bechamel sauce (butter, flour, milk, garlic powder, parsley, bay leaf, chili flakes, nutmeg, salt, pepper), Parmesan, homemade sausage (local pork, salt, pepper, fennel, oregano, sugar, parsley, chili flakes, granulated garlic), apples, and sage. Wood-fired. Garnished with snap pea micros.
dishes.guy_response: That is one delicious pizza. Great chew to the crust. Get a nice crackle on the outside with true gluten style flour pull. And this little bechamel Parmesan cheese program works completely right. Sausage still has good fat to it. A lot of great apple. The key at the end, though, that fresh herb on top. I can just go on and on about it. Outstanding.

restaurants.name: The Saucy Sicilian
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in OKC for a little pizza in the parking lot. Now, this isn't just a regular old pizza truck. No, this thing is a monster. And the oven, not any regular old oven. No, it's wood-fired. And the recipes, well, they're not your everyday recipes, because they're handed down from generations. This is the Saucy Sicilian.

dishes.description: Pizza dough (high-gluten flour, salt, water, yeast) mixed, balled, and proofed for three days. Topped with ricotta cheese (whole milk), mozzarella, fresh figs, roasted garlic, and roasted red pepper. Wood-fired in a pecan hickory oven for 90 seconds. Topped with prosciutto and drizzled with balsamic maple glaze (balsamic vinegar, pure maple syrup).
dishes.guy_response: I mean, it's just really delicious. The crust is the foundation, and it's out of bounds. Neapolitan pizza has to be in balance. And you give good portions, a good amount of prosciutto. It's an honest pizza. Scratch made, handmade, with a lot of family love in it. Well done.
